decisions.title: Architecture Pattern Selection: MVVM with SwiftData
decisions.rationale: SwiftUI pairs naturally with @Observable ViewModels. SwiftData for persistence provides native Apple framework with automatic CloudKit sync potential. Document-based app (NSDocument) gives open/save/save-as for free. Mind map state (selected node, expand/collapse, canvas transform) lives in MindMapViewModel (@Observable, @MainActor isolated). Node-specific state in NodeViewModel.

alternatives_considered.name: MVC with Core Data
alternatives_considered.description: Traditional MVC with NSController subclasses and Core Data
alternatives_considered.rejectedReason: Core Data is heavier, SwiftData is the modern Apple recommendation for new macOS apps

alternatives_considered.name: VIPER
alternatives_considered.rejectedReason: Overkill for a mind map app with ~10 screens. High ceremony, low productivity.

alternatives_considered.name: Flux/Redux
alternatives_considered.description: Unidirectional data flow with single store
alternatives_considered.rejectedReason: SwiftUI's @State/@Binding makes two-way binding natural. Redux adds boilerplate without benefit here.

decisions.tradeoffs: SwiftData is relatively new (2023) and less battle-tested than Core Data. Repository pattern (MindMapRepository protocol) provides abstraction for potential future migration.
decisions.bindingReason: Architecture decision, enforced through code review

decisions.title: Canvas Rendering: Viewport Culling with Spatial Index
decisions.rationale: Mind map with 10,000+ nodes requires O(log n) visibility queries. R-tree provides excellent performance for 2D spatial queries. Only nodes intersecting visibleRect are rendered as SwiftUI views. Off-screen nodes show placeholder dots. Connections culled when both endpoints off-screen.

alternatives_considered.name: LazyVGrid/LazyHStack
alternatives_considered.rejectedReason: These handle only linear layouts, not the 2D spatial layout of a mind map canvas

alternatives_considered.name: No culling (render all)
alternatives_considered.description: Simply render all nodes regardless of visibility
alternatives_considered.rejectedReason: FATAL: O(n) render at 10k nodes makes app unusable. BLOCKER-002.

alternatives_considered.name: Quadtree
alternatives_considered.rejectedReason: R-tree has better average performance for variable-density spatial data typical in mind maps

decisions.tradeoffs: R-tree requires additional library (swift-collections RTree or custom). Implementation adds complexity but is necessary for scale.
decisions.bindingReason: Performance requirement from BLOCKER-002 (FATAL), enforced through load testing

decisions.title: File Persistence: Atomic Writes with Backup
decisions.rationale: SwiftData ModelContext.save() with try? silently fails. BLOCKER-001 (FATAL): data loss on crash. Solution: write to temp location, verify, rename to final location. Before every save, copy current to .backup directory with timestamp. On launch, check store integrity.

alternatives_considered.name: SQLite WAL mode
alternatives_considered.rejectedReason: SwiftData doesn't expose low-level SQLite configuration. Not directly controllable.

alternatives_considered.name: NSDocument automatic versioning
alternatives_considered.rejectedReason: Designed for conflict resolution in multi-user scenarios, not crash recovery. Doesn't protect against mid-write corruption.

decisions.tradeoffs: Backup directory can grow large over time. Need cleanup policy (keep last 5 backups per document).
decisions.bindingReason: Data safety requirement from BLOCKER-001 (FATAL), enforced through crash simulation testing

decisions.title: Thread Safety: MainActor Isolation
decisions.rationale: SwiftData ModelContext is not thread-safe. BLOCKER-005 (HIGH): concurrent access causes CrossActorViolation. Solution: ALL ModelContext operations (save, fetch, insert, delete) run on @MainActor. Auto-save timer uses Task { @MainActor in } for main thread dispatch. MindMapViewModel is @MainActor isolated.

alternatives_considered.name: Actor-isolated ModelContext
alternatives_considered.rejectedReason: SwiftData ModelContext is already @MainActor constrained by Apple. Adding another layer adds complexity without benefit.

alternatives_considered.name: Serialize all context access via serial queue
alternatives_considered.rejectedReason: Works but less idiomatic than @MainActor. Doesn't integrate with SwiftUI's @State/@StateObject.

decisions.tradeoffs: All SwiftData work on main thread could cause jank during large saves. Mitigation: batch saves, background pre-processing of node data.
decisions.bindingReason: Concurrency safety from BLOCKER-005 (HIGH), enforced through -XactorIsolatedChecksStrict testing
